ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT HAJOCA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST THROUGH FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS' FAILURE TO ALLEGE A SPECIFIC PRODUCT ATTRIBUTABLE TO HAJOCA REQUIRES THE SUSTAINING OF THE DEMURRER. BOCKRATH REQUIRES THIS SPECIFICITY FOR PRODUCT LIABILITY CLAIMS AND CALIFORNIA COMMON LAW REQUIRES THIS SPECIFICITY FOR A FRAUD CLAIM. MOREOVER, AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, AN INDEPENDENT BASIS FOR SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER IS THAT PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO ALLEGE RELIANCE ON THE ALLEGED FRAUD. (CADLO). WHILE PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO ALLEGE ALL OF THE TARMANN REQUIREMENTS FOR THEIR FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, RELIANCE BY DECEDENT MUST STILL BE ALLEGED. MOREOVER, THAT CAUSE OF ACTION, BY COMBINING DISPARATE FRAUD THEORIES IS NEARLY INCOMPREHENSIBLE AND, FOR SAKE OF CLARITY AND NOTICE TO DEFENDANT, SHOULD BE REVISED. BECAUSE THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS DERIVATIVE OF THE FIRST FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED TO THAT CAUSE OF ACTION AS WELL. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, LAVENA WARD CSR #7707

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq., of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.
Laurel Halbany, Esq., of WATERS KRAUSE & PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Maria A. Navarro, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HIGHLAND STUCCO AND LIME PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Navarro, Sr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Atkinson's declaration are sustained. Defendant's objections to its own General Order interrogatory responses are overruled. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant's caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant's notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

Brendan J. Tuohy, Esq., of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Laurel Halbany, Esq., of WATERS KRAUSE & PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiff / opposing parties Maria A. Navarro, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Navarro was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant because Mr. Navarro, Jr.'s deposition testimony and defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories (regarding Cranite) preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However, defendant sustained its initial burden as to substantial factor causation through its material facts nos. 14 and 15 and plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue whether any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to decedent's risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hajoca Corporation's demurrer to first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second, third, fourth and ninth causes of action. As to the first through fourth causes of action, plaintiffs' failure to allege a specific product attributable to Hajoca requires the sustaining of the demurrer. Bockrath requires this specificity for product liability claims and California common law requires this specificity for a fraud claim. Moreover, as to the fourth cause of action, an independent basis for sustaining the demurrer is that plaintiffs failed to allege reliance on the alleged fraud. (Cadlo). While plaintiffs are not required to allege all of the Tarmann requirements for their fourth cause of action, reliance by decedent must still be alleged. Moreover, that cause of action, by combining disparate fraud theories is nearly incomprehensible and, for sake of clarity and notice to defendant, should be revised. Because the ninth cause of action is derivative of the first four causes of action, the demurrer is sustained to that cause of action as well. Judge: Harold Kahn, LaVena Ward CSR #7707. =(220/HEK)

Joinder In Deft Special Electric Company, Inc'S Mo To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Jun-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT THE PEP BOYS MANNY MOE & JACK OF CALIFORNIA's Joinder In Defendant Special Electric Company, Inc's Motion To Transfer Venue. No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Kentile Floors, Inc., The Pep Boys Manny Moe & Jack of California, Familian Corporation, and Keenan Properties, Inc.'s joinders to Special Electric Company's motion for change in venue are denied. Defendants have not shown that venue is improper under CCP 397(a) because they have not shown that all defendants reside outside San Francisco County. Nor have defendants demonstrated that McKesson Corporation is a 'sham defendant.' Defendants have not shown that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by a change in venue under CCP 397(c) because they have failed to provide a declaration setting forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each witness, and the reasons why the attendance of each witness would be inconvenient. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
